mnemonics.title: Display List Creation
mnemonics.content: NEE: glNewList → glEndList → Execute with glCallList. Think 'NEE' as 'New Entry Execute Exit'.

mnemonics.title: Matrix Stack Operations
mnemonics.content: PUSH to save, POP to restore. Remember: Push to Stack (Save), Pop from Stack (Restore).

mnemonics.title: Transformation Order
mnemonics.content: T-R-S: Translate, Rotate, Scale - Think 'The Rabbit Sleeps' - Transformations are applied in reverse order!

mnemonics.title: glScale Effects
mnemonics.content: Scale X: width, Scale Y: height, Scale Z: depth. Remember: XYZ = WHD (Width Height Depth).

acronyms.expansion: GL_COMPILE - Stores commands in list without executing during definition
acronyms.usage: Used when defining display lists to improve performance

acronyms.expansion: GL_MODELVIEW - Combined model and view matrix for object/camera transformations
acronyms.usage: Set matrix mode before applying modeling transformations

acronyms.expansion: Display List - Pre-compiled group of OpenGL commands for repeated execution
acronyms.usage: Use for static geometry rendered multiple times

visualTips.visualization: Think of a display list as a recipe card: Write commands while cooking (glNewList→glEndList = writing recipe), then execute anytime (glCallList = following recipe).
Imagine nested Russian dolls - glPushMatrix puts a doll inside, glPopMatrix takes it out. Each doll represents a transformation level.
Each transformation creates a new local coordinate system. Objects drawn after translation are positioned relative to that new origin.
Tree diagram: Root (body) → Branch (arm) → Leaf (hand). Moving the branch automatically moves the hand attached to it.

analogies.analogy: Like a macro in a spreadsheet: record a set of actions once, then replay with a single command whenever needed.
Like bookmarking a page in a book - you can return to exactly where you left off after reading other sections.
Like a car's assembly: the body is the parent, wheels are children. Move the body and wheels follow; move a wheel and only that wheel moves.
Like looking in a mirror - the X-axis reversal creates a mirror image of the object.
